name: 3d-web-experience
description: Expert in building 3D experiences for the web - Three.js, React Three Fiber, Spline, WebGL, and interactive 3D scenes. Covers product configurators, 3D portfolios, immersive websites, and bringing depth to web experiences. Use when: 3D website, three.js, WebGL, react three fiber, 3D experience.
qualityReason: Specific tech stack and use cases
capabilities: Implement Three.js 3D scenes and interactive experiences
Build React applications with React Three Fiber
Optimize 3D models and scenes for web performance
Integrate Spline designs into web projects
Add scroll-driven animations and interactions to 3D scenes
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation file containing markdown with code examples for 3D web development. All static findings are false positives: (1) The 'external_commands' detections are markdown code block delimiters misidentified as Ruby backticks; (2) The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are false matches on context words; (3) The single URL is a legitimate Spline CDN example. No actual security risks present.
valueStatement: Build stunning 3D websites and web apps using Three.js, React Three Fiber, and Spline. This skill helps you create interactive product configurators, immersive portfolios, and engaging 3D interfaces that work across all devices.
limitations: Does not create 3D models or assets - only implements them in code
Requires existing 3D assets (GLB/GLTF files) for full implementation
Performance depends on device capabilities - cannot override hardware limits

useCases.targetUser: E-commerce developers building product visualization features
useCases.title: E-commerce Product Viewer
useCases.description: Create interactive 3D product configurators that let customers rotate, zoom, and customize products before purchase.

useCases.targetUser: Web designers and creative professionals showcasing their work
useCases.title: Creative Portfolio Enhancement
useCases.description: Add impressive 3D elements to personal or agency portfolios to stand out from static websites.

useCases.title: Immersive Landing Pages
useCases.description: Build engaging landing pages with scroll-driven 3D animations that create memorable user experiences.

promptTemplates.title: Basic 3D Scene Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a basic 3D scene using Three.js with a rotating cube, ambient lighting, and orbit controls. Explain the key components.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new 3D web project from scratch

promptTemplates.title: React Three Fiber Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to integrate a GLB 3D model into a React app using React Three Fiber. Include loading state and error handling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding a 3D model to an existing React application

promptTemplates.title: 3D Product Configurator
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a product configurator that lets users change colors and textures on a 3D model. Include UI controls outside the canvas.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating an e-commerce product customization feature

promptTemplates.title: Scroll-Driven 3D Animation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a 3D scene where camera movement and model rotation are controlled by scroll position. Use R3F ScrollControls.

bestPractices: Always include loading states and progress indicators for 3D content
Test 3D experiences on real mobile devices, not just desktop browsers
Provide static image fallbacks for users on low-end devices or with reduced motion preferences
antiPatterns: Adding 3D elements purely for visual effect without functional purpose
Implementing 3D that only works on desktop browsers
Failing to provide any loading feedback while 3D content loads

faq.question: What is the best way to start learning 3D web development?
faq.answer: Start with Spline for quick results if you are new to 3D. Move to React Three Fiber when you need more control. Learn vanilla Three.js last for maximum flexibility.

faq.question: How do I make my 3D site work on mobile?
faq.answer: Test on real devices. Reduce model complexity. Lower pixel ratio on mobile. Consider disabling 3D on very low-end devices. Always provide fallback content.

faq.question: What 3D model formats work best for web?
faq.answer: GLB and GLTF are the standard formats. They are small, well-supported, and include everything in one file. Avoid FBX or OBJ for web.

faq.question: How do I handle 3D loading times?
faq.answer: Show progress with useProgress hook. Display skeleton or placeholder. Lazy load 3D after main content. Compress models aggressively.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with any JavaScript framework?
faq.answer: React Three Fiber works with React. For Vue, use TroisJS or Vue-Threejs. For vanilla JS, use Three.js directly. Spline works in any framework.

faq.question: How do I add scroll-driven animations to my 3D scene?
faq.answer: Use @react-three/drei ScrollControls. Access scroll.offset in useFrame to drive animations. Combine with GSAP ScrollTrigger for advanced effects.
